title: Tunnel Screen
desc: Debris from Lake Ken-I-Po keeps snagging on the screen above Everrain's hidden tunnel mouth.

steps.text:
One of Everrain's stranger chores begins with surface leaves. The hidden tunnel connecting this cavern to Lake Ken-I-Po above draws in whatever the upper water happens to be carrying - reeds, twigs, seed husks, smooth stones, the occasional fish bone, once a whole abandoned paddle. Most of it catches on the iron screen set above the tunnel mouth before it can clog the narrower passages behind it.

Today the screen basket is heavy enough to slow the current at the inlet. A work crew has the shore winch ready and a pair of long hook poles laid out beside the ledge. "If the flow backs up," the crew lead says, "this whole side cave goes ankle-deep by noon and waist-deep by evening. We clear it now or regret it later."

You can work the crank that raises the basket out of the water, or take a hook line to the lower shelf and free the biggest snags by hand before the crew lifts.

options.label: Work the shore winch and raise the basket
Take a hook pole to the lower rock shelf

steps.text:
The winch fights you at first. Waterlogged debris has a stubborn kind of weight, and the screen basket rises inch by inch through the black-green surface before finally breaking free in a rush of dripping reeds and lake weed.

What comes up with it looks like a message from another world. Pale surface leaves, broad and clean. A shell polished almost white. A knot of rushes that still smells faintly of open air instead of cave damp. UnderWorld workers pick through the heap with quick practiced hands, muttering about how the lake above never stops sending them chores.

Once the basket is clear, the current smooths at once. Water begins slipping cleanly through the bars toward the tunnel again. The crew lead lifts one of the pale leaves between two fingers and snorts. "Pretty," he says. "Troublesome, but pretty." That seems to describe the relationship between Everrain and the surface above it as well as anything.

options.label: Help sort the debris and reset the screen

steps.text:
The lower shelf sits just above the waterline, slick with spray and narrow enough to make every step feel like a decision. From here the tunnel mouth is only a few feet away: a dark pull beneath the ledge where current slides downward into secrecy.

You use the hook pole to draw loose the worst of the snags before the crew raises the basket. A branch comes free first, then a twisted rope of lake weed, then a smooth stone the size of your palm worn round by water that has seen both worlds. Each piece you free changes the sound at the inlet, from choked slap to clean steady draw.

When the basket finally comes up, the crew lead looks over the cleared mouth with approval. "Good reach," he says. "Any deeper and I'd have had to lend you a line, and then we'd both be wet and irritated." The tunnel keeps taking water from the world above. Everrain keeps making that fact manageable.

options.label: Climb back up with the hook pole
